description: ShopSense - AI Shopping Assistant demo app for Groundcover DevRel. A microservices architecture (Python/FastAPI) with 4 services designed to generate rich observability telemetry: distributed traces, LLM call monitoring, inter-service communication patterns, and chaos injection for demo scenarios.

userStories.title: Scaffold monorepo with FastAPI boilerplate
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the basic project structure with all 4 FastAPI services so I can start building features.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory structure: services/gateway/, services/catalog/, services/recommendation/, services/orders/
Each service has a main.py with a FastAPI app, health check endpoint at GET /health returning {status: ok, service: <name>}
Each service has requirements.txt with fastapi, uvicorn, httpx
Root docker-compose.yml that builds and runs all 4 services on ports 8000-8003
Each service has a Dockerfile using python:3.11-slim
Root .gitignore for Python projects
All services start without errors via docker-compose up

userStories.title: Product Catalog Service - models and CRUD
userStories.description: As a user, I need a product catalog so I can browse items in the shop.
acceptanceCriteria: Product model with fields: id, name, description, price, category, image_url, stock
In-memory store with 20+ seed products across categories (electronics, clothing, home, food)
GET /products - list all products with optional category filter query param
GET /products/{id} - get single product
GET /products/search?q=<query> - text search across name and description
All endpoints return JSON with proper status codes
Typecheck passes (use type hints throughout)
userStories.notes: Use Pydantic models. Keep it simple with in-memory storage - no database needed for demo.

userStories.title: Order Service - cart and checkout
userStories.description: As a user, I need to add items to a cart and check out so I can complete a purchase flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Cart model: id, items (product_id, quantity, price), created_at
POST /cart - create a new cart, returns cart_id
POST /cart/{cart_id}/items - add item to cart (validates product exists by calling catalog service)
GET /cart/{cart_id} - get cart with items and total
POST /cart/{cart_id}/checkout - process checkout, returns order confirmation
Checkout calls catalog service to verify stock and get current prices
All inter-service calls use httpx with proper error handling
userStories.notes: Inter-service calls to catalog service at http://catalog:8001. This creates distributed traces.

userStories.title: Recommendation Service - LLM-powered suggestions
userStories.description: As a user, I want AI-powered product recommendations so I can discover relevant items.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /recommend endpoint accepting {product_ids: [], preferences: string}
Calls catalog service to fetch full product details for context
Uses OpenAI-compatible API (configurable base_url and api_key via env vars) to generate recommendations
Falls back to simple keyword matching if LLM is unavailable
Returns top 5 recommended products with reasoning
Includes timing metadata in response (llm_latency_ms, total_latency_ms)
GET /recommend/health includes LLM connectivity status
userStories.notes: Use OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_BASE_URL env vars. Default to a mock/fallback mode for easy demo without real API key.

userStories.title: API Gateway - request routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single entry point that routes to all backend services.
acceptanceCriteria: Proxy routes: /api/products/* -> catalog service, /api/cart/* -> order service, /api/recommend/* -> recommendation service
GET /api/health returns aggregated health from all services
CORS middleware allowing all origins (demo app)
Request logging middleware that logs method, path, status, duration_ms
Proper error handling when downstream services are unavailable (503 with service name)
userStories.notes: Gateway is the single entry point. All client requests go through it.

userStories.title: Chaos engineering endpoints
userStories.description: As a DevRel presenter, I need to inject failures and latency so I can demo Groundcover's alerting and trace analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Each service gets a POST /chaos/latency endpoint to add artificial delay (ms parameter)
Each service gets a POST /chaos/error endpoint to make the service return errors (rate parameter 0-1)
Each service gets a POST /chaos/reset to clear all chaos settings
GET /chaos/status returns current chaos configuration
Chaos affects all subsequent requests until reset
Gateway gets POST /api/chaos/scenario endpoints for pre-built scenarios: 'slow-checkout', 'flaky-recommendations', 'cascade-failure'
userStories.notes: This is KEY for demos. Pre-built scenarios make live presentations smooth.

userStories.title: Load generator for demo traffic
userStories.description: As a DevRel presenter, I need realistic traffic patterns so Groundcover dashboards show interesting data.
acceptanceCriteria: Python script at scripts/loadgen.py
Simulates user journeys: browse products -> search -> get recommendations -> add to cart -> checkout
Configurable: --rps (requests per second), --duration (seconds), --gateway-url
Generates a mix of successful and error-prone requests
Prints summary statistics when done (total requests, error rate, p50/p95/p99 latency)
Can run continuously or for a fixed duration
userStories.notes: This generates the telemetry data that makes Groundcover dashboards look great during demos.

userStories.title: Kubernetes manifests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need K8s manifests so I can deploy to a cluster where Groundcover is installed.
acceptanceCriteria: k8s/ directory with manifests for all 4 services
Each service has: Deployment (2 replicas), Service (ClusterIP), resource limits
ConfigMap for shared configuration (service URLs, feature flags)
Gateway gets a LoadBalancer or NodePort service for external access
Namespace: shopsense
Labels on all resources: app, service, version (for Groundcover service map)
Kustomization.yaml for easy deployment
userStories.notes: Proper K8s labels are important - Groundcover uses them for service map visualization.

userStories.title: Simple web frontend
userStories.description: As a demo viewer, I want a visual UI so the demo feels like a real app, not just API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Single HTML file served by the gateway at GET /
Product grid showing items from catalog (fetch from /api/products)
Search bar that calls /api/products/search
Click product to see AI recommendations (calls /api/recommend)
Add to cart button and cart view with checkout
Chaos control panel (collapsible) to trigger demo scenarios
Clean, modern CSS (no framework needed, just looks decent)
userStories.notes: Keep it simple - single HTML file with vanilla JS. This is for demos, not production.

userStories.title: README with demo walkthrough
userStories.description: As a DevRel user, I need clear docs on how to run the demo and what to show in Groundcover.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md with: project overview, architecture diagram (ASCII), quick start (docker-compose up), K8s deployment guide
Demo script section: step-by-step walkthrough for a live demo
What to show in Groundcover section: service map, distributed traces, LLM monitoring, chaos scenarios
Environment variables reference table
Screenshots placeholder section
userStories.notes: The README is critical for DevRel - it's what people see first on GitHub.
